reasoning:
The forecast assesses the likelihood that Federal Reserve primary credit borrowing will exceed its December 17, 2025 value (8870) on three different future report dates. Historically, primary credit displays a secular upward trend driven by QT, draining reserves and increasing structural dependence on the discount window throughout 2025 (+19.85/day linear trend, or ~140/week). However, it exhibits a distinct intra-quarter seasonality, marked by massive borrowing spikes at quarter-ends (seen in late June and September) and broad troughs mid-quarter (seen in July and October).

1. **Resolution 2025-12-28 (references Dec 24 data)**: The series is currently in a Q4 ramp-up phase. Tracking precisely with Q2 and Q3 patterns, the final week before quarter-end reliably experiences another jump as market liquidity squeezes. Expect Dec 24 to spike even higher than Dec 17. (Probability: 0.65)
2. **Resolution 2026-01-20 (references Jan 14 data)**: Mid-quarter periods invariably see sharp drops from quarter-end peaks. The January trough will likely pull borrowing heavily below the Dec Q4 peak, overcoming the underlying baseline trend for a few weeks. (Probability: 0.38)
3. **Resolution 2026-03-21 (references Mar 18 data)**: Although far enough out to carry high uncertainty, the secular trajectory indicates the baseline level of borrowing will shift up by another ~1500–2000 compared to Q4. As Q1 quarter-end approaches in March, borrowing should exceed the December Q4 peak. (Probability: 0.58)

The probabilities strictly respect the horizon uncertainty rule, moving progressively closer to 0.5 for temporally distant intervals.
